ObjectivesExpected OutcomesSyllabusEvaluationText Books and References
Objective:
The objective of this course is to understand database management
systems in detail and how to implement in business applications.
More emphasis is provided on how to organize, manage and store
data in data bases using DBMS efficiently, and effectively.
Dr. Pramod Yelmewad  IIITDCS266December 30, 20253 / 12

ObjectivesExpected OutcomesSyllabusEvaluationText Books and References
Expected Outcomes
On completion of the course you will learn:
Understand the fundamental concepts of data, database, DBMS
Explain the basic concepts of relational data model,
entity-relationship model, relational database design, relational
algebra and SQL.
Use ER-model concepts and design few database applications,
Convert the ER-model to relational tables, populate relational
database.
Apply query language SQL and formulate different SQL queries
on data.
Use ACID properties of DBMS and Improve the database design
by normalization.
Familiar with basic database storage structures and access
techniques: file and page organizations, indexing methods
including B tree, and hashing.
Dr. Pramod Yelmewad  IIITDCS266December 30, 20254 / 12

ObjectivesExpected OutcomesSyllabusEvaluationText Books and References
Module 1
Introduction to database systems:
need of databases, data models, relational databases, database
design, and database architecture components.
Dr. Pramod Yelmewad  IIITDCS266December 30, 20255 / 12

ObjectivesExpected OutcomesSyllabusEvaluationText Books and References
Module 2
Relational database model
Basic SQL:Overview of SQL Query Language, Data Definition, Basic
Query Structure, Additional Basic Operations, Set Operations, Null
Values, Aggregate Functions, Nested Subqueries, Modification of the
Database.
Intermediate SQL: Join Expressions, Views, Transactions, Integrity
Constraints, SQL Data Types and Schemas.
Advanced SQL: JDBC and ODBC, Stored functions and procedures,
Triggers.
Dr. Pramod Yelmewad  IIITDCS266December 30, 20256 / 12

ObjectivesExpected OutcomesSyllabusEvaluationText Books and References
Formal Query Language
Formal Query Language
Relational Algebra, Tuple Relational Calculus, Domain Relational
Calculus
Dr. Pramod Yelmewad  IIITDCS266December 30, 20257 / 12

ObjectivesExpected OutcomesSyllabusEvaluationText Books and References
Module 4
Database Design
ER Model: Model design, entity, entity set, relationship set, and
mapping cardinality.
Normalization: Decomposition techniques: lossy and lossless,
functional dependencies, canonical cover. Normal forms: 1NF, 2NF,
3NF, BCNF
Dr. Pramod Yelmewad  IIITDCS266December 30, 20258 / 12

ObjectivesExpected OutcomesSyllabusEvaluationText Books and References
Module 5
Transaction Management
ACID properties, transaction states, concurrent executions,
schedules, conflict and view serializabilities.
Dr. Pramod Yelmewad  IIITDCS266December 30, 20259 / 12
